steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 2 argues that Luttrell's conflict with Sempringham Priory was rooted in a property dispute and that he believed he had a rightful claim to the property. The question asks what Text 2's historian would say is necessary for a fair assessment of Luttrell.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — [ignores the justification angle]: This sidesteps Text 2's core point about Luttrell's belief in his own right. ✗
  • Choice B — [introduces unrelated points]: Text 2 does not argue that outside factors unrelated to property rights shaped Luttrell's conduct. ✗
  • Choice C — [misrepresents the historian's view]: Text 2 does not endorse Luttrell's actions unconditionally — it argues his belief in justification must be considered. ✗
  • Choice D — 'a fair assessment must consider Luttrell's belief that his actions were justified': This matches Text 2's central claim that Luttrell acted on what he believed was his rightful claim. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
